title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Browser and Node Client Systems · Narrowing Workshop
summary: An accessibility product team integrating a staging browser API must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for response status, success variants, failure taxonomy, and problem details, producing typed response and failure classifier. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch typed response and failure classifier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Handle 201, 202, 204, 206, and other success responses with their location, body, and completion implications. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Status Families
steps.instruction:
workshop case — accessible staging dashboard SDK observes 202 without a result, 204 with no content, 206 with a range, 429 with Retry-After, and 503 problem+json for case T-2. Preserve status, transport, schema, domain, partial, and unknown-effect outcomes separately.

What must be established before implementation?
